claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 9 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 5 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for, and if so, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit. I have two children - Alex, who's 9, and Blake, who's 5.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! You have two children - Alex (age 9) and Blake (age 5).

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you a few questions:

Do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! Now, do Alex and Blake both live with you in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they both live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

Are you or your partner (if you have one) currently receiving Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake already?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody is currently claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. One final question:

Are either Alex or Blake currently in hospital, care, or any other living arrangement away from home for an extended period?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they're both at home with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Good News - You Are Eligible! 

Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake**.

### Here's why:

**For Alex (age 9):**
- Alex is under 16 years old
- Alex lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for Alex
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- Alex is not in hospital or care

**For Blake (age 5):**
- Blake is under 16 years old
- Blake lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for Blake
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake
- Blake is not in hospital or care

You meet all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. You can proceed with making a claim for both children.

### Important to note:
If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, you can still claim Child Benefit even if this applies to you.

Would you like any additional information about how to make your claim?

reasonings: Alex is 9 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, and you are responsible for them. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and Alex is not in hospital or care. You meet all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 5 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, and you are responsible for them. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Blake, and Blake is not in hospital or care. You meet all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for two children (Alex, age 9, and Blake, age 5) who both live with them full-time. Both children are under 16 years old, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child, and neither child is in hospital or care. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for both children.
